LAKEWOOD YACHT CLUB SEAHORSE NEWSLETTER
Volume 41, Issue 3 | March 2024

FROM THE HELM
Commodore Patricia Harmon

Dear members and sailing families,

Spring is here and Lakewood Yacht Club is buzzing with activity. Our junior
sailing program is underway and we are proud to announce that the club has
established a new sailing scholarship fund for families with financial need.
Three families received assistance in the spring 2024 season.

I'd like to thank outgoing Junior Sailing Director Mike Castillo, who served
the program for seven outstanding years. His dedication to our young sailors
has shaped countless careers and lifelong passions for the sea.

JUNIOR SAILING NEWS
Youth Program Spring 2024

Spring registration is now open for all junior sailing programs. This year we
welcome back Coach Maria Santos, who joined our team last fall after coaching
at Austin Yacht Club for three seasons. Coach Santos will lead the Opti fleet
alongside veteran coach David Nguyen.

The C420 fleet introduced this season has exceeded expectations. We have 12
sailors enrolled in the new C420 division, bringing our junior fleet total to
52 sailors across all divisions.

Key dates for junior families:
- March 9: Orientation for new junior sailors and families
- March 16: First on-water session begins
- April 13: Intramural regatta (parents welcome to spectate)
- May 11: Texas Youth Race Week team selection

SCHOLARSHIP FUND
The new LYC Junior Sailing Scholarship Fund was formally established in January
2024. The fund is supported by member donations and provides partial and full
tuition assistance for junior sailing programs. Applications are reviewed by
the junior sailing committee on a rolling basis. Contact the office for details.

RACING RESULTS — MARCH
Galveston Bay Winter Series, Final Results

PHRF A Fleet:
1. Persistence (J/105) — Bill Murray
2. Windfall (Hunter 40) — Tom Davis
3. Sea Serpent (J/35) — Anne Garcia

Optimist Green Fleet:
1. Finn Harmon
2. Lily Nguyen
3. Carlos Santos

The Seahorse racing team finished 2nd overall at the Bay Area Junior League
invitational held March 2-3 at TCYC. Nine LYC sailors competed across Opti,
C420, and ILCA divisions.

UPCOMING EVENTS
Spring Calendar

March 9: Junior Sailing Orientation — all new families encouraged to attend
March 16: First junior sailing session on the water
March 23: Spring Work Party — volunteers needed to prepare racing marks
April 5: Good Friday cruise — all members welcome, dinner at 6pm
April 20: Easter egg hunt for club children (families with children under 12)
May 4: Spring regatta — Galveston Bay Open
May 25: Memorial Day cruise and cookout

MEMBER NEWS

Welcome to our newest members: the Castellanos family (Seabrook), the Kim
family (Friendswood), and the Okonkwo family (League City). All three families
have children enrolled in the junior sailing program.

In memoriam: We mourn the loss of long-time member George Patterson, who
sailed with LYC for over 30 years. A memorial sail will be held April 13.

Fair winds,
Patricia Harmon, Commodore
